summary: Create sources/manifest.toml with all v1 sources
description:
## Goal

Create the source registry manifest that tracks all v1 corpus sources with their download URLs, license status, and extraction instructions.

## Output File

**sources/manifest.toml**

## TOML Schema

```toml
[[source]]
id = "gospel-of-thomas"                    # unique identifier (lowercase, hyphenated)
tradition = "gnosticism"                   # tradition category
label = "Gospel of Thomas (Lambdin translation)"  # human-readable title
url = "http://gnosis.org/naghamm/gthlamb.html"   # source URL
format = "html"                            # html | html_multi | text | sefaria_api
license = "public_domain"                  # all v1 sources are public domain
translator = "Thomas O. Lambdin"           # translator name
notes = "Full Coptic-to-English translation. Scrape main content div."  # extraction hints
```

## v1 Sources to Include

### Gnosticism (gnosis.org)
- **gospel-of-thomas**: http://gnosis.org/naghamm/gthlamb.html (Thomas O. Lambdin)
- **gospel-of-philip**: http://gnosis.org/naghamm/gphlamb.html (Thomas O. Lambdin)

### Hermeticism (sacred-texts.com)
- **corpus-hermeticum-01** through **corpus-hermeticum-17**: https://www.sacred-texts.com/chr/herm/index.htm (G.R.S. Mead)
  - Note: Index page links to individual tractates. Use format=html_multi

### Buddhism (sacred-texts.com)
- **heart-sutra**: https://www.sacred-texts.com/bud/index.htm (Edward Conze)

### Jewish Mysticism (Sefaria API)
- **sefer-yetirah**: Sefaria API (public domain)
  - Use format=sefaria_api
- **zohar-leid**: Selections from Zohar (public domain)
  - Use format=sefaria_api

## Format Field Values

| format | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| html | Single page, extract main content |
| html_multi | Index page with links to individual text pages |
| text | Direct plaintext download |
| sefaria_api | Hit Sefaria public API |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All v1 sources from docs/guru-implementation.md Stage 1 are in manifest.toml
- [ ] Each entry has complete fields (id, tradition, label, url, format, license, translator, notes)
- [ ] Format field matches actual source type
- [ ] Notes field includes extraction hints for each source
- [ ] TOML is valid and parseable

resolution.note: Created sources/manifest.toml with 23 v1 sources: 2 gnostic texts (gnosis.org), 17 Corpus Hermeticum tractates (sacred-texts.com), 1 Heart Sutra (sacred-texts.com), 2 Jewish mysticism texts (Sefaria API)
